User.title: User
User.description: Represents an internal user of the SaaS platform with specific roles and access levels (e.g., Admin, Financeiro, Comercial). Authentication data is managed externally by Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Email address of the user, used for communication and as a unique identifier for Firebase Authentication.

role.description: Assigned role of the user within the system, determining access permissions. (e.g., 'Admin', 'Financeiro', 'Comercial').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the user's information.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer of the digital solutions company, including their contact information, contracted services, and overall status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

fullName.description: Full name of the client contact person or primary representative.

companyName.description: Legal name of the client's company.

documentNumber.description: CPF (for individuals) or CNPJ (for companies) of the client.

email.description: Primary email address for client communication.

whatsapp.description: WhatsApp contact number for the client.

zipCode.description: Postal code (CEP) of the client's address.

serviceIds.description: References to Service entities that this client has contracted. (Relationship: Client N:N Service)

notes.description: Internal observations or remarks about the client.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the client's information.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines the types of digital solutions offered by the company (e.g., professional websites, custom applications, management systems).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: Name of the service offered (e.g., 'Sites profissionais', 'Aplicativos personalizados').

defaultPrice.description: Optional default pricing for the service, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the service's information.

Contract.title: Contract
Contract.description: Manages details of recurring service agreements with clients, including financial terms, dates, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Contract entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client entity associated with this contract. (Relationship: Client 1:N Contract)

serviceId.description: Reference to the Service entity provided by this contract. (Relationship: Service 1:N Contract)

monthlyValue.description: The recurring monthly value of the contract.

startDate.description: The date when the contract officially started.

endDate.description: The date when the contract is scheduled to end, if applicable. Can be null for open-ended contracts with automatic renewal.

billingFrequency.description: The frequency at which the client is billed (e.g., 'Monthly', 'Quarterly', 'Annually').

status.description: Current operational status of the contract (e.g., 'Active', 'Overdue', 'Suspended', 'Canceled', 'Finished').

contractPdfId.description: Reference to an Upload entity containing the contract PDF document. (Relationship: Upload 1:1 Contract)

signedContractPdfId.description: Reference to an Upload entity containing the digitally signed contract PDF. (Relationship: Upload 1:1 Contract)

lastPaymentDate.description: The date of the last successful payment received for this contract. This can be derived from invoices.

nextInvoiceDate.description: The calculated date for the next expected invoice generation for this contract.

notes.description: Internal observations or remarks about the contract.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the contract record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the contract's information.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Records individual billing statements generated for a contract, tracking amount due, due date, and payment status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

contractId.description: Reference to the Contract entity this invoice is associated with. (Relationship: Contract 1:N Invoice)

amount.description: The total amount due for this invoice.

issueDate.description: The date when the invoice was generated and issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which this invoice is expected to be paid.

paymentStatus.description: Current payment status of the invoice (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Canceled').

paymentMethod.description: The intended payment method for this invoice (e.g., 'PIX', 'Credit Card', 'Bank Slip', 'Manual').

proofOfPaymentId.description: Reference to an Upload entity containing proof of payment (e.g., bank transfer receipt), if applicable. (Relationship: Upload 1:1 Invoice)

paidDate.description: The date when the invoice was successfully paid.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the invoice's information.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records individual payment transactions received, linking them to a specific invoice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice entity this payment is applied to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N Payment)

amountPaid.description: The actual amount paid in this transaction.

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was received.

method.description: The method used for this payment (e.g., 'PIX', 'Credit Card', 'Bank Slip', 'Manual').

transactionId.description: Optional unique transaction identifier from the payment gateway or bank.

notes.description: Internal observations or remarks about the payment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

Upload.title: Upload
Upload.description: Manages metadata for files stored in Firebase Storage, such as contract PDFs, signed documents, or payment proofs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Upload entity.

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'image/jpeg').

fileSize.description: Size of the uploaded file in bytes.

storagePath.description: The path or URI to the file's location within Firebase Storage. This is the primary way to access the file.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the User entity who uploaded this file. (Relationship: User 1:N Upload)

linkedEntityType.description: The type of entity this upload is associated with (e.g., 'Contract', 'Invoice', 'Client').

linkedEntityId.description: The unique identifier of the entity this upload is linked to.

description.description: A brief description or context for the uploaded file.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded and this record was created.

ActivityLog.title: ActivityLog
ActivityLog.description: Records significant actions and changes within the system for audit trails, client history, and activity timelines.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity who performed the activity. (Relationship: User 1:N ActivityLog)

description.description: A detailed description of the activity that occurred.

entityType.description: The type of entity primarily affected by this activity (e.g., 'Client', 'Contract', 'Invoice').

entityId.description: The unique identifier of the entity affected by this activity.

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the activity took place.

changes.description: JSON string representing the changes made, if the activity involved data modification (e.g., '{ "oldValue": "active", "newValue": "suspended" }').

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Stores system-generated alerts and messages for internal users (e.g., reminders for upcoming payments, contract expirations, or new leads).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity who should receive this notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

type.description: The category or nature of the notification (e.g., 'PaymentDue', 'ContractExpiring', 'InvoiceOverdue', 'NewLead').

message.description: The full content of the notification message.

targetUrl.description: A URL within the application that the user can navigate to for more details related to the notification.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the user has marked the notification as read.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was generated.

triggeredByEntityId.description: Optional reference to the ID of the entity that triggered this notification (e.g., contractId, invoiceId).

triggeredByEntityType.description: Optional type of the entity that triggered this notification (e.g., 'Contract', 'Invoice').

CRMLead.title: CRMLead
CRMLead.description: Manages potential clients (leads) within a basic CRM system, tracking their status and progress through the sales pipeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CRMLead entity.

fullName.description: Full name of the lead contact person.

companyName.description: Company name associated with the lead.

email.description: Email address of the lead for communication.

whatsapp.description: WhatsApp contact number for the lead.

source.description: Where the lead originated from (e.g., 'Website', 'Referral', 'Cold Call').

status.description: Overall status of the lead (e.g., 'New', 'Contacted', 'Qualified', 'Proposal Sent', 'Converted', 'Lost').

pipelineStage.description: The current stage of the lead in the sales pipeline (e.g., 'Initial Contact', 'Discovery', 'Proposal', 'Negotiation').

notes.description: Detailed notes and history of interactions with the lead.

assignedToUserId.description: Reference to the User entity (Commercial role) responsible for this lead. (Relationship: User 1:N CRMLead)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lead record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the lead's information.

ScheduleEntry.title: ScheduleEntry
ScheduleEntry.description: Records appointments, meetings, reminders, and events for internal users, potentially linked to clients or leads.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ScheduleEntry entity.

title.description: A concise title for the scheduled event or task.

description.description: Detailed description of the scheduled event.

type.description: The category of the schedule entry (e.g., 'Meeting', 'Reminder', 'Follow-up', 'Event').

startTime.description: The start date and time of the event.

endTime.description: The end date and time of the event.

assignedToUserIds.description: References to User entities who are participants or responsible for this schedule entry. (Relationship: User N:N ScheduleEntry)

relatedClientId.description: Optional reference to a Client entity if the event is related to a specific client. (Relationship: Client 1:N ScheduleEntry)

relatedLeadId.description: Optional reference to a CRMLead entity if the event is related to a specific lead. (Relationship: CRMLead 1:N ScheduleEntry)

location.description: Physical or virtual location of the event (e.g., 'Google Meet', 'Client Office').

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the scheduled event or task has been completed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the schedule entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the schedule entry's information.

CompanySetting.title: CompanySetting
CompanySetting.description: Stores global configuration settings for the company operating the SaaS, such as company details, branding, and billing preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CompanySetting entity. Expected to be a fixed ID like 'companyProfile' as there will likely be only one record.

companyName.description: The official name of the company.

logoUrl.description: URL to the company logo, typically stored as an Upload entity.

emailSenderName.description: The name used as the sender for automated emails (e.g., 'NexusFlow Pro').

emailSenderAddress.description: The email address used as the sender for automated emails.

emailSignature.description: Standard email signature to be appended to automated emails.

billingExternalSetupDetails.description: Placeholder for information about external billing system setup or sensitive keys (e.g., payment gateway API keys, bank account details for PIX/Boletos). *Note: Sensitive data like API keys should not be stored directly in Firestore documents; refer to secure server-side secrets management practices.*

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the company settings record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the company settings.

definition.description: Stores internal user profiles for the SaaS platform, including basic information and the user's role (for application-level logic/UI). Access is path-based: a user can read/write their own profile (matching `request.auth.uid` with `{userId}`). Admins have full access to manage all user profiles.

Dedicated collection for assigning 'Admin' role permissions. The existence of a document at this path signifies that `userId` has Admin privileges. Used by security rules for 'Existence over Content' DBAC, avoiding `get()` calls on the /users collection for role checks. Only Admins can create or delete documents here.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user assigned to the Admin role, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for assigning 'Financeiro' role permissions. The existence of a document at this path signifies that `userId` has Financeiro privileges. Used by security rules for 'Existence over Content' DBAC. Only Admins can create or delete documents here.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user assigned to the Financeiro role, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for assigning 'Comercial' role permissions. The existence of a document at this path signifies that `userId` has Comercial privileges. Used by security rules for 'Existence over Content' DBAC. Only Admins can create or delete documents here.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user assigned to the Comercial role, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores global configuration settings for the company operating the SaaS. Expected to contain a single document (e.g., with ID 'companyProfile'). Access is restricted to users with the 'Admin' role.

params.name: settingsId
params.description: The unique ID for the company settings document (e.g., 'companyProfile').

definition.description: Defines the types of digital solutions offered by the company. Provides a lookup for contract types. Read access for Admin, Financeiro, and Comercial roles. Write access is restricted to the Admin role.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Service entity.

definition.description: Stores comprehensive information about each client. Access is controlled by Admin, Financeiro, and Comercial roles, with specific role-based permissions defining read/write capabilities across all client records.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Client entity.

definition.description: Manages details of recurring service agreements with clients. Includes denormalized `clientId`, `clientCompanyName` (from Client), and `serviceName` (from Service) for efficient querying, UI display, and authorization independence. Access is controlled by Admin, Financeiro, and Comercial roles.

params.name: contractId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Contract entity.

definition.description: Records individual billing statements generated for a contract. Includes denormalized `clientId`, `contractId`, `clientCompanyName`, `contractMonthlyValue`, and `contractStatus` to facilitate security rules and queries for specific clients/contracts without requiring `get()` calls. Access is controlled primarily by Admin and Financeiro roles.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

definition.description: Records individual payment transactions received. Includes denormalized `invoiceId`, `contractId`, `clientId`, `invoiceAmount`, and `invoicePaymentStatus` to link payments directly to client/contract context for robust security rules and efficient reporting. Access is controlled primarily by Admin and Financeiro roles.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Payment entity.

definition.description: Manages metadata for files stored in Firebase Storage (e.g., contract PDFs, proofs of payment). Includes denormalized `uploadedByUserId`, `linkedEntityType`, `linkedEntityId`, `linkedClientName`, and `linkedContractId` for explicit access control and contextual linking to parent entities without requiring `get()` calls. Access is role-based, potentially allowing users who uploaded or are related to the linked entity to access.

params.name: uploadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Upload entity.

definition.description: Records significant actions and changes within the system for audit trails and timelines. Includes denormalized `userId` (the actor), `userFullName`, `entityType`, `entityId`, and `entityName` (contextual name) for clear logging and simplified access rules. Primarily read-only for Admin role, with potential filtered read access for other roles based on the affected entity.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ActivityLog entry.

definition.description: Manages potential clients within the basic CRM system. Includes denormalized `assignedToUserId`, `assignedToUserFullName`, and `assignedToUserEmail` to facilitate access control. Access is granted to Admin role and 'Comercial' users who are explicitly assigned (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.assignedToUserId`). Client-side queries are expected to filter by `assignedToUserId` for Comercial users.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the CRMLead entity.

definition.description: Records appointments, meetings, and events. Includes denormalized `assignedToUserIds` (an array for multiple participants), `assignedToUserNames`, `relatedClientId`, `relatedClientName`, `relatedLeadId`, and `relatedLeadName` for context and simplified access rules. Access is granted to Admin role or users whose `request.auth.uid` is present in the `assignedToUserIds` array. Client-side queries are expected to filter by `assignedToUserIds` for non-Admin users.

params.name: scheduleEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ScheduleEntry entity.

definition.description: Stores user-specific system-generated alerts and messages. This subcollection structure enforces path-based ownership, ensuring strict QAPs for 'list' operations: only the user matching `{userId}` in the path (i.e., `request.auth.uid == userId`) can access their notifications. Includes denormalized `userId` and `userFullName` of the recipient for clarity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user to whom this notification belongs, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Notification entity.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database Access Control), QAPs (Query As Permission), and Invariants. This design aims to create a highly secure, scalable, and debuggable system for NexusFlow Pro.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies and avoid `get()` calls in security rules, crucial authorization context is denormalized directly into the documents. For instance, `invoices` documents will contain `clientId` and `contractId` from their respective parent entities, allowing rules to directly check relationships without needing to fetch the parent `contract` or `client` document. Similarly, `payments` documents will denormalize `invoiceId`, `contractId`, and `clientId`. For user-specific data like notifications, a subcollection (`/users/{userId}/notifications/{notificationId}`) ensures that authorization is inherently tied to the path, making rules simple and atomic.

For role-based access control (DBAC), instead of relying on a `role` field within the main `/users/{userId}` document (which would necessitate a `get()` call for every role check outside of the user's own profile), dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{userId}`, `/roles_financeiro/{userId}`, and `/roles_comercial/{userId}` are used. Security rules will check for the *existence* of a document at these paths (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`), which is an atomic and efficient operation, fully adhering to the 'Existence over Content' principle for global roles. The `role` field in the `/users/{userId}` document will primarily serve application-level logic and UI display.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports secure `list` operations by either using path-based ownership or by denormalizing necessary authorization fields. 

1.  **Path-Based Ownership for Private Data:** For truly private and user-specific data, such as `notifications`, a hierarchical path (`/users/{userId}/notifications/{notificationId}`) is employed. This ensures that any `list` query on `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/notifications` will inherently only return documents owned by the authenticated user, satisfying QAPs as the path itself acts as the filter.

2.  **Structural Segregation and Denormalization for Shared Data:** For collections where multiple roles or specific users might have access (e.g., `clients`, `contracts`, `crmLeads`, `scheduleEntries`), the documents themselves contain fields like `assignedToUserId`, `assignedToUserIds` (for `crmLeads` and `scheduleEntries`, respectively), or `clientId`/`contractId` (for `invoices`, `payments`). Security rules for `list` operations on these top-level collections will typically allow authenticated users (`allow list: if request.auth.uid != null;`). However, the client-side queries MUST include explicit `where` clauses (e.g., `where('assignedToUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)` for `crmLeads`, or `where('assignedToUserIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)` for `scheduleEntries`). The `read` and `write` rules will then enforce the actual authorization based on the denormalized fields, ensuring that even if a `list` query *could* retrieve more data, unauthorized individual documents cannot be read or modified.

**Clarity of Intent and DBAC:** The use of dedicated role collections (`/roles_admin`, etc.) clearly expresses the authorization model. All authorization decisions are based on `request.auth.uid` and data explicitly stored or denormalized within the accessed document, making security rules straightforward to write, understand, and debug.

**Invariants:** Timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`) are consistently present across entities to track data integrity. Ownership and relational integrity are maintained through explicit `userId`, `clientId`, `contractId`, etc., fields, often denormalized where necessary to prevent costly `get()` operations in rules.
